methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Growth → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.2x) to $690M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Bethlehem Steel Corp is a Growth-stage robotics and manufacturing company that has raised $690M to date. The comparable universe consists of 9 private peers (including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics) and 1 public company, though the private peer set lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage clustering to support a reliable peer-median valuation approach.

The Stage-Progression method is the primary applicable valuation framework. Using total capital raised of $690M as a proxy baseline and applying a 1.2x step-up multiplier typical for Growth-stage advancement, this method implies a valuation of $828M. The Peer-Median approach was not feasible; while 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, none met the threshold of three or more datapoints within the same stage, adjacent stage, or similar valuation tier needed for statistical reliability.

The triangulated valuation range is $704M to $952M, with a median of $828M. This narrow range reflects reliance on a single applicable method rather than cross-validation across multiple approaches. Key uncertainties include the representativeness of the 1.2x step-up multiplier for this specific company and stage, the absence of direct peer benchmarking, and limited visibility into capital efficiency and revenue metrics. A fuller valuation would benefit from additional private comparable transactions or clarification of the company's revenue and growth trajectory.
